Taotao He

Associate Professor of Management Science
Shanghai Jiao Tong University
hetaotao (at) sjtu.edu.cn


About Me

Hello! Welcome to my website! I am an associate professor without tenure at Antai College of Economics and Management, Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU). I received my Ph.D. in 2019 from the Quantitative Methods at Mitchell E. Jr., Daniels School of Business, Purdue University, where I was advised by Professor Mohit Tawarmalani.

My research interests are in all aspects of mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP), including theory, algorithms, software and applications. I am particularly interested in developing convexification techniques and efficient algorithms for these problems. Recently, I am also interested in using ideas from MINLP to improve decision-making in operations problems involving consumer choice behavior.

Here is my SJTU webpage in Chinese.

Research Papers [Google Scholar]

  1. Assortment Optimization under History-Dependent Effects
    Taotao He, Yating Zhang, and Huan Zheng (2024)
    Operations Research, under review.
    PDF
  2. Discrete Nonlinear Functions: Strong Formulations and Apllications
    Taotao He and Mohit Tawarmalani (2024)
    Operations Research, under review.
    PDF
  3. Convexification Techniques for Fractional Programs
    Taotao He, Siyue Liu, and Mohit Tawarmalani (2023)
    Mathematical Programming, under review.
    PDF
  4. An Integer Programming Approach for Quick-Commerce Assortment Planning
    Yajing Chen, Taotao He, Ying Rong, and Yulong Wang (2023)
    Management Science, under review.
    PDF
  5. MIP Relaxations in Factorable Programming
    Taotao He and Mohit Tawarmalani (2024)
    SIAM Journal on Optimization, forthcoming.
    PDF Code
  6. Tractable Relaxations of Composite Functions
    Taotao He and Mohit Tawarmalani (2022)
    Mathematics of Operations Research.
    PDF
  7. A New Framework to Relax Composite Functions in Nonlinear Programs
    Taotao He and Mohit Tawarmalani (2021)
    Mathematical Programming.
    PDF

Teaching

  • Optimization Methods in Economics: 2020 - 2024 Spring
  • Integer Programming and Combinatorial Optimization: 2021 - 2024 Spring
  • Deterministic Operations Research: 2023 Fall
  • Management Science: 2020 Fall

Personal

I love playing soccer. Now, I play for SJTU Football Team. At Prudue, I was a member of Purdue Chinese Football Club.
